framings.neutral: Person A, Person B, and Person C finished a race. The person who finished second is not the tallest. The tallest person finished before the shortest person. Who finished last?
framings.western: John, Michael, and Sarah finished a race. Michael is the tallest. Sarah is the shortest. The person who finished second is not the tallest. The tallest finished before the shortest. Who finished last?
framings.diverse: Ravi, Amara, and Yuki finished a race. Amara is the tallest. Yuki is the shortest. The person who finished second is not the tallest. The tallest finished before the shortest. Who finished last?

framings.neutral: All members of Group A like jazz. Person X is a member of Group A. Does Person X like jazz?
framings.western: All members of the book club like jazz. Emily is a member of the book club. Does Emily like jazz?
framings.diverse: All members of the study group like jazz. Priya is a member of the study group. Does Priya like jazz?

framings.neutral: Person A has twice as many apples as Person B. Together they have 12 apples. How many apples does Person B have?
framings.western: Jake has twice as many apples as Lily. Together they have 12 apples. How many apples does Lily have?
framings.diverse: Kwame has twice as many apples as Mei. Together they have 12 apples. How many apples does Mei have?

framings.neutral: Person A, B, and C sit in a row. Person A does not sit next to Person C. Person B sits between the other two. Who sits in the middle?
framings.western: Alice, Michael, and Grace sit in a row. Alice does not sit next to Grace. Michael sits between the other two. Who sits in the middle?
framings.diverse: Soo-Jin, Tariq, and Fatima sit in a row. Soo-Jin does not sit next to Fatima. Tariq sits between the other two. Who sits in the middle?

framings.neutral: Every object in Box 1 is red. Object Z is blue. Is Object Z in Box 1?
framings.western: Every item in Tom's collection is red. The vase is blue. Is the vase in Tom's collection?
framings.diverse: Every item in Aiko's collection is red. The vase is blue. Is the vase in Aiko's collection?

framings.neutral: Person A is 3 years older than Person B. The sum of their ages is 27. How old is Person A?
framings.western: David is 3 years older than Claire. The sum of their ages is 27. How old is David?
framings.diverse: Obinna is 3 years older than Leila. The sum of their ages is 27. How old is Obinna?

framings.neutral: Three people—A, B, C—each prefer a different color: red, blue, green. A does not like blue or green. B does not like red. What color does C like?
framings.western: Three people—Bob, Carol, Dan—each prefer red, blue, or green. Bob does not like blue or green. Dan does not like red. What color does Carol like?
framings.diverse: Three people—Jin, Hana, Kofi—each prefer red, blue, or green. Jin does not like blue or green. Kofi does not like red. What color does Hana like?

framings.neutral: No member of Team X has a car. Person Q is a member of Team X. Does Person Q have a car?
framings.western: No member of the hiking club has a car. Rachel is a member of the hiking club. Does Rachel have a car?
framings.diverse: No member of the chess club has a car. Yara is a member of the chess club. Does Yara have a car?

framings.neutral: Person A has 5 more books than Person B. Person A has 8 books. How many books does Person B have?
framings.western: Anna has 5 more books than Ben. Anna has 8 books. How many books does Ben have?
framings.diverse: Fatou has 5 more books than Hiroshi. Fatou has 8 books. How many books does Hiroshi have?

framings.neutral: Person A finished ahead of Person B. Person C finished behind Person A but ahead of Person B. What place did Person C finish?
framings.western: Laura finished ahead of Mark. Nina finished behind Laura but ahead of Mark. What place did Nina finish?
framings.diverse: Chioma finished ahead of Daisuke. Esperanza finished behind Chioma but ahead of Daisuke. What place did Esperanza finish?

framings.neutral: All fast objects are loud. Object M is fast. Is Object M loud?
framings.western: All fast cars are loud. Peter's car is fast. Is Peter's car loud?
framings.diverse: All fast cars are loud. Amara's car is fast. Is Amara's car loud?

framings.neutral: Person A and Person B together have 10 coins. Person A has 3 more coins than Person B. How many coins does Person A have?
framings.western: Sam and Alex together have 10 coins. Sam has 3 more coins than Alex. How many coins does Sam have?
framings.diverse: Nia and Taro together have 10 coins. Nia has 3 more coins than Taro. How many coins does Nia have?

framings.neutral: Person A is taller than Person B. Person B is taller than Person C. Who is the tallest?
framings.western: Tom is taller than Lisa. Lisa is taller than Greg. Who is the tallest?
framings.diverse: Bongani is taller than Elif. Elif is taller than Chloe. Who is the tallest?

framings.neutral: Some members of Club X play guitar. Person R is a member of Club X. Does Person R definitely play guitar?
framings.western: Some members of the debate club play guitar. Kevin is a member of the debate club. Does Kevin definitely play guitar?
framings.diverse: Some members of the art club play guitar. Zara is a member of the art club. Does Zara definitely play guitar?

framings.neutral: Person A earns twice as much per hour as Person B. Person A works 5 hours and earns $50. How many hours must Person B work to earn $50?
framings.western: Mia earns twice as much per hour as Joe. Mia works 5 hours and earns $50. How many hours must Joe work to earn $50?
framings.diverse: Amara earns twice as much per hour as Kenji. Amara works 5 hours and earns $50. How many hours must Kenji work to earn $50?

framings.neutral: If it rains, Person A brings an umbrella. It is raining. Does Person A have an umbrella?
framings.western: If it rains, Sophie brings an umbrella. It is raining. Does Sophie have an umbrella?
framings.diverse: If it rains, Yemi brings an umbrella. It is raining. Does Yemi have an umbrella?

framings.neutral: A, B, and C each scored differently on a test. A scored higher than C. B scored lower than C. Who scored the lowest?
framings.western: Alice, Carol, and Bob each scored differently. Alice scored higher than Bob. Carol scored lower than Bob. Who scored the lowest?
framings.diverse: Lena, Hana, and Omar each scored differently. Lena scored higher than Omar. Hana scored lower than Omar. Who scored the lowest?

framings.neutral: Person A has 3 times as many stickers as Person B. They have 24 stickers total. How many does Person B have?
framings.western: Emma has 3 times as many stickers as Ryan. They have 24 total. How many does Ryan have?
framings.diverse: Aisha has 3 times as many stickers as Dmitri. They have 24 total. How many does Dmitri have?

framings.neutral: Everyone in Building Z owns a pet. Person T lives in Building Z. Does Person T own a pet?
framings.western: Everyone in the apartment complex owns a pet. James lives in the apartment complex. Does James own a pet?
framings.diverse: Everyone in the dormitory owns a pet. Nadia lives in the dormitory. Does Nadia own a pet?

framings.neutral: Person A only eats at Restaurant X on Fridays. Today is Wednesday. Is Person A eating at Restaurant X?
framings.western: Carlos only eats at the diner on Fridays. Today is Wednesday. Is Carlos eating at the diner?
framings.diverse: Mei only eats at the noodle shop on Fridays. Today is Wednesday. Is Mei eating at the noodle shop?
